name: d4-site-template
description: Base Next.js site shell: layout, header/footer with a nav slot system, home/about/contact pages, contact form with email delivery, theme via CSS variables. Every assembled site starts from this template; feature modules copy in on top of it.
clientFacingSummary: A fast, modern marketing site with a home page, about page, and a contact form that delivers inquiries to your inbox.

env.name: RESEND_API_KEY
env.description: Resend API key for contact form email delivery. If unset, submissions are stored in data/messages.json instead.

env.name: CONTACT_TO_EMAIL
env.description: Destination inbox for contact form submissions when RESEND_API_KEY is set.

env.name: TURSO_DATABASE_URL
env.description: libsql:// URL for this client's Turso database, used as the contact-form fallback store when RESEND_API_KEY is unset. Falls back to a local SQLite file under data/cms.db in dev.

env.name: TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN
env.description: Auth token for TURSO_DATABASE_URL. Required whenever TURSO_DATABASE_URL is set.

env.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL
env.description: Canonical public origin (https://clientdomain.com) used by sitemap.xml and robots.txt. Falls back to the Vercel deployment URL, then localhost.

postAssemble.notes: The builder rewrites src/config/nav.generated.ts with nav entries from every selected module, rewrites src/config/site.ts values from the build config, rewrites src/app/theme.css when a theme/preset/pairing supplies one, and rewrites src/config/fonts.generated.ts + src/config/design.generated.ts (font pairing + motion signature) when the build config names a pairing from d4-site-builder's pairings.json. The checked-in defaults for both are the modern-signal pairing so the template runs standalone.
